Q4 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which one of the following statement is FALSE for streamlines in a steady incompressible flow?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Converging straight streamlines pattern indicates convective tangential deceleration in the flow direction.

Q6 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A triangular gate with height 6 m and base of 1 m lies in a vertical plane with vertex downwards. The bottom vertex of the gate is 7 m below the free surface of the tank which contains a liquid of specific gravity of 0.7. Assuming acceleration due to gravity to be 10 m/s2, the hydrostatic force exerted by the liquid on the gate is _______ kN (Correct upto one decimal place)

NAT
Correct answer: 63.0
Solution

63.0 (60.0 - 64.0)

Q7 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A liquid in a hydraulic cylinder is compressed to decrease its volume by 0.16 percentage. If the pressure of the liquid is increased from 80 N/cm2 to 128 N/cm2, the bulk modulus of elasticity of liquid is ______ N/mm2 (Answer in integer)

NAT
Correct answer: 300
Solution

300 (298 - 302)

Q8 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

An air bubble of diameter 1 mm is formed in water at a location 0.5 m below the free surface. Considering surface tension at water and air interface to be 0.08 N/m, the gauge pressure inside the air bubble is _____ Pa. (Answer in integer)

NAT
Correct answer: 5225
Solution

5225 (5220 - 5230)

Q9 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

With increase in temperature, which of the following statement(s) regarding viscosity is/are CORRECT?
 

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, b, c)
• With increase in temperature cohesive forces between the liquid molecules decreases resulting in decrease in viscosity of liquids.
• With increase in temperature molecular collisions between the molecules of gases increases resulting in increase in viscosity of gases.
• Kinematic viscosity of liquid may increase or decrease.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

NAT
Correct answer: 2291
Solution

Solution

598.2 (595.0 - 604.0)
